rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving named things to valid expressions with types; avoid introducing new architecture.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
Ensure AGREE declarations use only visible identifiers and types from the current AADL scope.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the correct scalar types: int, real, bool.
If a guarantee references an invalid identifier, trace the root cause and either fix the reference or remove the clause.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE unless they are present in the model's dependencies.
Validate that all AGREE expressions reference only features visible in the current component scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes should contain assumptions and guarantees over visible ports; implementation annexes handle subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture or package references when fixing AGREE diagnostics; only modify existing visible identifiers.
Do not assume that unresolved references can be fixed by inventing new declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about expression types in component type annexes
